content.problemStatement:
For any zero-indexed sequence SSS of length NNN, define a function G(S)G(S)G(S) as follows:

G(S)=‚àèi=0N‚àí1(Si+i)G(S) = \prod_{i=0}^{N-1} (S_i + i)G(S)=i=0‚àèN‚àí1‚Äã(Si‚Äã+i)If N=0N = 0N=0, then G(S)=1G(S) = 1G(S)=1, i.e. the empty sequence has a value of 111.

You have an array BBB, which is initially empty.

You are also given an array AAA of length NNN, as well as a parameter KKK.

For each iii from 111 to NNN in order, you must insert AiA_iAi‚Äã at the **front** of array BBB.

So, after the iii-th insertion, BBB will look like [Ai,Ai‚àí1,Ai‚àí2,‚Ä¶,A1][A_i, A_{i-1}, A_{i-2}, \ldots, A_1][Ai‚Äã,Ai‚àí1‚Äã,Ai‚àí2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,A1‚Äã].

After each insertion, consider all sequences SSS of the form

S=(Bi1,Bi2,‚Ä¶,Bim)S = (B_{i_1}, B_{i_2}, \dots, B_{i_m})S=(Bi1‚Äã‚Äã,Bi2‚Äã‚Äã,‚Ä¶,Bim‚Äã‚Äã)
such that 1‚â§i1<i2<‚ãØ<im‚â§‚à£B‚à£1 \le i_1 < i_2 < \dots < i_m \le |B|1‚â§i1‚Äã<i2‚Äã<‚ãØ<im‚Äã‚â§‚à£B‚à£ and 0‚â§m‚â§K0 \le m \le K0‚â§m‚â§K.

In other words, SSS is a subsequence of BBB of size at most KKK.

Your task is to compute ‚àëG(S)\sum G(S)‚àëG(S) over all such subsequences SSS.

Since the answers can be very large, output them modulo 998‚Äâ244‚Äâ353998\,244\,353998244353.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK ‚Äî the number of integers being inserted, and the upper bound on the length of subsequences under consideration.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots,  A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã




Output Format
For each test case, output NNN lines - the iii-th line must contain the answer after iii insertions, modulo 998‚Äâ244‚Äâ353998\,244\,353998244353.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases doesn't exceed 10510^5105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111:

After the first insertion, B=[5]B = [5]B=[5].
Its subsequences are [],[5][], [5][],[5], so the answer is 1+5=61 + 5 = 61+5=6.
After the second insertion, B=[4,5]B = [4, 5]B=[4,5].
Its subsequences are [],[4],[5],[4,5][], [4], [5], [4, 5][],[4],[5],[4,5], so the answer is 1+4+5+4‚ãÖ(5+1)=341 + 4 + 5 + 4\cdot (5+1) = 341+4+5+4‚ãÖ(5+1)=34.
